- 17 Quick Tips for Low Carb Dieting in the Real World!: Kind: captions Language: en eating a low-carb diet is an effective way to lose weight but we live in a high-carb world that is filled with fast food temptations grab-and-go pastries and endless pizza commercials in this video i'll share 17 quick tips to help you stay low-carb if you're just getting started this list will carry you all the way through to your goal if you're an established low carb dieter keep track and see how many of these tips you've already mastered one of the first things to realize when you go low carb is that carb addiction is real when you eat refined carbs like cookies cakes candies cereal and bread your brain releases a chemical called dopamine dopamine is nicknamed the feel good chemical because it makes you feel good did you ever eat a stale piece of chocolate or a dry pastry and think yuck this doesn't even taste good only to go on and eat the entire thing well that is thanks to the stimulation of dopamine your mouth might think that it doesn't taste good but your brain is loving it the only way to break free of carb addiction is to cut the carbs however like other addictive substances when you give it up you're faced with a few days of withdrawal symptoms to feel better follow tip number two which is to take an electrolyte supplement carbohydrates cause your body to retain water when you cut the carbs water along with electrolytes like sodium potassium and magnesium are flushed out which adds to the fatigue craving and headaches that we see with withdrawal do you know what else makes you feel better eating a refined carbohydrate but if you try to take the edge off of by sneaking a high carb snack you'll set back your progress be patient and allow your body the time it needs to transition from being a good sugar burner to being a good fat burner and to avoid giving into temptation live by tip number four which shares the truth that all cravings pass whether you give in and eat junk food or not the desire will go away as i like to say the only thing that chocolate does for me is make me want more chocolate of course anyone can get caught off guard by a weak moment so if you have an unplanned splurge follow tip number five and get right back on track with your next meal not the next day the quicker you get back on course the better you'll feel and the less work you'll make for yourself in the future tip number six is that refined carbs and fat don't mix the reality is that sometimes it stinks to be a low-carb dieter because the real world is not set up for your success we use high carb treats like cake to celebrate happy occasions and pizza is the ultimate quick dinner but if we break these foods down we see that they are a mix of refined carbohydrates and dietary fat which is the best combination to gain body fat the refined carbs in these calorie dense foods spike insulin which is the hormone that shuttles the unneeded calories into fat storage while eating carbs and fat together is not good doing the opposite should also be avoided so tip number seven is to avoid eating a diet that is low in both carbohydrates and dietary fat when you do that you rob your body of both of its primary energy sources and you feel drained and hungry the formula for a low carb or keto diet is to keep your carbohydrate intake low your fat intake high and your protein in a moderate range tip is to simplify your carb choices by focusing on the best and avoiding the worst the best option is non-starchy vegetables and the worst is sugar so for the most success think salad greens broccoli and cauliflower and skip those sugary snacks and desserts if you want a complete list of low carb foods that are acceptable you can download this list of 100 through the links provided tip number nine you'll be happiest with your results if you fill up at meal time and limit snacking the time off between meals allows your insulin level to drop which promotes the release of fat from your fat cells to help you avoid snacking at night follow tip number 10 which is to eat a high fat low carb dinner this combination will help you feel full until bedtime and keep your insulin level low making it easier for your body to burn fat overnight the best way to stay in control of your dinner is through our next tip which is to cook at home when you cook at home you know what's in your food cooking at home also allows you to gather some favorite recipes which makes long-term success easier a couple of high-fat low-carb meals that are easy to prepare include grilling meats and vegetables and having breakfast for dinner if you do find yourself at a restaurant go prepared with tip number 12 which is to pick your meal before you enter the most restaurants have online menus with the nutritional facts listed take 10 minutes before you go out to eat to choose what you would like to eat when you get to the restaurant don't even bother opening the menu you've already made the best choice when you cook at home and pre-pick your restaurant meals you find it easier to follow tip number 13 which is to avoid carb creep carb creep is a term i use to describe the tendency to loosen the rules on your low carb diet allowing your daily carbs to creep up if you're just getting started it's a good idea to track your carb intake daily if you are an experienced low carb dieter you may not need to track your intake every day but by doing so every week or two you can avoid carb creep to make sure you're not going down a slippery slope with your food choices follow tip number 14 to determine if the item is worth eating to do this make sure your intended meal meets two criteria you want the food to taste good and leave you feeling good after you eat it if it doesn't pass that test don't eat it as you get into your low carb journey you must guard against discouragement one of the best things to do is keep in mind tip number 15 which shares that comparisons are dangerous things your body is the one you've got and it is doing the best it can with the resources that it has available others might be able to eat more carbohydrates than you or get faster results but as long as you stick to your diet and keep your focus you will reach your goal another thing to guard against is the judgment of others which brings us to tip number 16. others will always question why you've changed your eating habits but there's no need to defend something that works results speak loudly stick with your diet and in the future some of those naysayers might just come to you for dieting advice and this ties into our 17th and final tip don't stop before the magic happens whether you have 10 pounds to lose or hundreds of pounds to lose the transition will take time except that the best things in life are worth working for and keep your focus on your goal there's nothing better for your joints heart immune system and sense of satisfaction than freeing yourself from the burden of excess weight and your low carb diet will get you where you want to be as long as you don't give up okay there you go 17 ways for you to stay low carb in this high carb world before you go please click the subscribe button and hit the bell icon to get a notification when my next video is released thanks for watching

because even in my training as a dietician and a certified diabetes educator that's like you know the gold standard of educators in diabetes care they don't talk about this they just assumed ketosis means diabetic ketoacidosis and it's a medical emergency and the sky is falling so nutritional ketosis is when your body burns primarily fat for fuel because the diet is limited in carbohydrates but not energy so you're eating enough food in the form of fat and protein you're just not eating a ton of carbohydrates in this state your blood sugar remains normal this is a natural state that pregnant women will go in and out of throughout pregnancy it's benign it's nothing to worry about you're eating enough calories you're getting enough micronutrients you're just not binging on carbohydrates shunt as many nutrients to the baby because that's when the baby is growing very rapidly meaning in terms of like putting on mass and you also start breaking down maternal fat stores so early in - This Is What a Diabetes-Friendly Diet Looks Like | Diabetes Meal Plan for Beginners | Prep School: Kind: captions Language: en being diagnosed with diabetes can bring on a whole roller coaster of emotions but one thing you don't have to worry about is what to eat we have you covered meal planning and meal prep can go a long way to make healthy eating easier during the busy week and the meal prep muffin tin egg recipe we're going to make today is one of my favorites to make these muffin to omelets are so great because you can make a batch at the beginning of the week grab them from the fridge for breakfast or snacks and they're so delicious so to start we need to whisk some eggs together and we're going to use eight eggs here so eggs as we all know are a great protein Source protein is really important for everyone especially for people with diabetes because protein digests slowly which means our blood sugars will stay more stable after a meal so a single egg delivers six grams of protein per serving and you're wanting to get a few servings of protein on each meal to give you that nice satisfaction that you want at the end of a meal any scrambled egg you need a little milk in here so we're just going to use a quarter teaspoon of salt here so people with diabetes need to be aware of how much salt they're eating in addition to saturated fat those two nutrients are important because they matter to our heart and people with diabetes are at a higher risk for heart related complications and then a quarter teaspoon of pepper and we can start whisking away all right so sometimes we're going to add some nice fresh flavor and we need about quarter of a cup if you don't like chives if you don't have chives you can add some cilantro or scallions or even dried herbs and spices which are nice and convenient Pantry Staples push the chives out of the way here make room for a muffin cup we're going to spray these with a little non-stick cooking spray so they release easily and if you haven't already preheat your oven to 325. okay so here's the trick rather than adding all of your mix-ins into your eggs and then trying to pour it all out evenly a better way of doing this is just to divide your ingredients evenly throughout the muffin cups and this is also a stage where you could do three of one flavor three of another half and half really whatever your heart desires I'm going to start with some broccoli we're just going to evenly distribute this throughout our muffin tin next up ham so ham we know it has some lovely saltiness to it we're just going to use a little bit a little bit goes a long way to add so much flavor this ham also helps to add some protein as well so you're going to get a really nice satisfying breakfast out of this now we move on to our cheese which also adds a little bit of saltiness so we really tested this recipe many times so that we had just the right amount of saltiness without being too much for people with diabetes so this is where the measuring cup comes in handy because you can pour directly out of here so we're just going to evenly distribute this egg mixture throughout don't fill it to the very very top because these eggs really do fluff up and expand once in the oven okay last step here is adding some chives on top for some color and flavor all set to go into the oven 325 degrees Fahrenheit for about 20 to 25 minutes oh here we are 20 minutes in the oven and they are ready we're gonna wait for these egg muffins to cool off a little bit and then you can either pop them in one big meal prep container to go in the fridge or portion them out separately so really all you have to do in the morning is grab your bag and go now this is what a healthy day of eating for a diabetes friendly diet looks like we have our meal prepped muffin tin eggs and a whole day of delicious eats I'm gonna dish out breakfast all it needs to do is be heated up and then enjoyed so for breakfast we have these - Starting Low Carb? Eat This Today | Full Day of Eating: Kind: captions Language: en-US To follow a low carb diet, you must reduce the number of carbohydrate grams you eat in a day. That is an obvious statement, but what does that look like, and what can you eat? In this video, I share a full day of eating a low carb diet for those who are just getting started. [Intro Music] There are many things that cause confusion when you start a low carb diet. One of the main issues is how many grams of carbs to eat? There is low carb dieting, and there is keto dieting. The difference is the level of carbohydrates. While the cut-off points are not fully agreed upon, you can think of a low carb diet as a day of eating that does not contain more than 125 total grams of carbohydrates. To be in the keto range, you need to drop your carb grams to no more than 50 total carbs for the day. For this video, I am going to show you a daily diet that splits those values. These foods in front of me add up to 75 total grams of carbohydrates. One way to start your low carb day of eating is with a smoothie that contains low-sugar fruits with a good fiber-to-carb ratio. Avocado and strawberries fit those criteria. For instance, to make this smoothie, I placed half of an avocado and a half cup of frozen strawberries into a blender along with a cup of almond milk, a handful of spinach, and a tablespoon of almond butter. And, I will share the ingredients for each of these foods in the video. You can also get the recipes on my blog post that goes with this video. I will share a link to my blog below the video in the description area. This smoothie contains 23 grams of carbs, but almost 10 of those carbs are from fiber. That is a good fiber to carb ratio. That fiber is going to slow the absorption of the smoothie, keeping hunger away longer. The slow absorption will also prevent the blood sugar and insulin spike you get when you eat something refined for breakfast, like a muffin. When insulin is high, it prevents the release of fat from the fat cells. So, right from the start of the day, you have a convenient food that tastes good and keeps your blood sugar steady. As you are stepping into your low carb lifestyle, there is no better meal to include than a salad, and there are a couple of great reasons for that. For one, salad greens are low in carbs but provide a lot of hunger-satisfying volume. For instance, this salad has about four cups of greens but only six carbs, four of which are fiber. Also, a salad provides you with the perfect vehicle to bring healthy fats into your daily diet. One of the biggest mental hurdles you will face when you shift to low carb eating is allowing yourself to increase the amount of fat you eat. We were told for a year that eating fat makes you fat, and it is hard to get away from that mindset. However, adding healthy, high-quality fats to your diet helps your body transition away from a primarily carb-burning metabolism to a fat-burning metabolism. This salad is topped with many healthy fats including, the other half of the avocado from breakfast, a hard-boiled egg, feta cheese crumbles, walnuts, and a full-fat salad dressing made with avocado oil. That adds up to 36 grams of fat. The salad also contains a few slices of apple that provide a crunch and a bit of sweetness. The apple and greens contribute most of the 22 grams of carbohydrates and 9 of fiber in this lunchtime salad. For dinner, stir fry is a quick and easy choice for beginners, especially if you utilize frozen stir fry vegetables and a pre-cooked rotisserie chicken from the grocery store. To make this, I simply cooked a bag of frozen vegetables in a tablespoon of avocado oil, added the pre-cooked chicken and a quick homemade sauce. One thing that may surprise you when you start a low carb diet is that many sauces and condiments contain sugar and high-fructose corn syrup. Those simple carbs can sabotage your weight loss. Fortunately, you can often whip up a homemade, low-carb version. For instance, this stir fry sauce contains a quarter cup of chicken broth, 2 tablespoons of soy sauce, and 1 tablespoon of red wine vinegar. As you transition to a low carb lifestyle, yogurt is something that can be enjoyed as a dessert or snack. However, you need to choose yogurt carefully. The vast majority of single-serve yogurts in the dairy aisle of your grocery store are low in fat. When they removed the fat, they removed the flavor, which they replace with sugar or sweeteners. To support your low carb lifestyle, you will want to choose plain, full-fat yogurt and then add your own ingredients at home. Higher milk fat yogurt may only be available in larger containers, but they keep well in your refrigerator, even after opening. This yogurt dessert contains a quarter cup of frozen blueberries that I microwaved for 30 to 45 second and then pressed to extract the juices. I then added a half cup of yogurt, a half teaspoon of vanilla, and a pinch of pure Stevia. Now, This video is being made for YouTube, which is a broad audience. The meals in front of me add up to just over 1,200 calories, which will be low for some, but this works well for demonstration purposes because it is easier to add low-carb/high-fat items rather than subtract them. When we breakdown the macros for this day of eating, we see that it contains 75 total grams of carbohydrates, making up 25 percent of the daily calories with another 25 percent coming from protein and just over 50 percent of the day's calories coming from healthy fats. Many of you will find this macronutrient breakdown to be a comfortable starting place if you are coming from a high-carb lifestyle. It allows you to step into a low carb diet rather than dive into a very low carb/keto When you start this way, you may still experience some fatigue as your body adapts, but lessen many of the "keto-flu" symptoms that result from a more dramatic change. Now, I would like to draw your attention to a couple of things about this low carb menu in front of me. First of all, there are three small servings of fruit: blueberries, strawberries, and slices of apple. When you are just getting started on your low carb lifestyle, you may find that your body tolerates some fruit. However, everyone's tolerance for fruit is different, so you be happiest with your long-term results if you limit your fruit intake. I have many videos that discuss fruit on my channel if you would like to explore that topic further. You will also notice that there is no bread item like toast or a bagel. A slice of whole-wheat toast contains about 16 grams of carbohydrates. If you are looking to bring your carb intake below the upper threshold of 125 grams, you could technically include a piece of toast in your diet. However, bread is refined, so it will get absorbed into your bloodstream quickly, causing the blood sugar and insulin spikes that encourage fat storage. There ARE breads that are advertised as low-carb or no-carb. This is accomplished by manipulating the ingredients to reduced the net carb count. On paper, that seems ideal. However, these processed foods tend to be absorbed into the body quickly, and depending on what has been added, may cause an increase in blood sugar and insulin. When processed foods are utilized, counting net carbs rather than total carbs can easily become a trap that derail your weight loss. That is why I am sharing the total carb content for this low carb menu. You will also notice that this menu is made up of whole foods. There are some whole foods that are higher in carbs that can be worked into a beginner's low carb diet due to their high content of fiber and micronutrients. For instance, this salad does not contain beans. However, if you are just moving your diet into the low-carb range, you can make them work. Beans are whole, nutrient-dense foods, but they also contain a fair amount of carbohydrates. To give you an idea, a half cup of black beans contains about 22 grams of carbs and nine grams of fiber. If you account for those carbohydrates and you enjoy beans, you can work them into your overall daily diet. I hope you see from this video that a low-carb diet made up of whole foods is enjoyable and effective for weight loss. I provided a few examples here, but there are many more healthy low-carb foods to keep you feeling satisfied. I have a list of 100 low carb foods displayed on a single pdf that you can download and print for free from my website. I will provide links to the food list here on the video and below in the description area. Please subscribe to my channel and hit the bell icon to get notified when my new videos are released. Thanks for watching and enjoy the rest of your day.

- 5 Low Carb, High-Fat Foods to Eat Every Day: Kind: captions Language: en A low carb, high-fat diet keeps hunger away and moves your body into a fat burning state. In this video, I'm going to share five lowcarb, high-fat foods that you can easily add to your daily diet. Hi, I'm Dr. Becky from drbeckyfitness.com. I'm a college instructor of the science of nutrition. Let me start with the avocado. Um, avocados contain a lot of calories which can scare people off. Uh, for instance, this avocado weighs just over 200 gram, which means that it contains more than 300 calories, but most of those calories come from healthy monounsaturated fats. And those fats along with um the 13 or more grams of fiber in an avocado satisfy your hunger and they slow down your digestion, which makes it easier for you to consume fewer calories over the course of your day. Um, now I will say that you don't need to eat a whole avocado to reap the benefits. Um, add half of an avocado to a smoothie or uh put it as a salad topper and you'll not only get those healthy fats, you'll also get a healthy dose of vitamins and minerals that will support your metabolism and your overall health. Next, let's go to the much maligned egg. You know, as a society, we are still trying to recover from the years that we were taught that eggs are high in cholesterol and therefore bad for us. Uh while it is true that an egg contains a lot of cholesterol, the notion that eating cholesterol is a main cause of heart disease has been disputed. Um, even the US dietary guidelines that came out in 2015 changed their stance on cholesterol and now say that cholesterol is not considered a nutrient of concern for overconumption. And another study which was published in the same year shows that even people with metabolic issues can eat a high egg diet without consequences. The study shows that people with type 2 diabetes were able to eat 12 eggs a week with no significant change to their total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, or triglycerides compared to a group that ate just two eggs a week. So, the takeaway is that you can feel free to have at least an egg a day. When you do, you'll get a whole host of micronutrients, including choline, which uh will help with brain function. So, it combats things like brain fog and memory issues. Um, and an egg a day will also keep hunger away. Uh, and that's due to the great mix of proteins and fats that you get from eggs. Um, of course, they're also very versatile, right? So, you can fry them up for breakfast. You can make them into a kiche for dinner, which is something that we often do in in our home. Uh, and hard-boiled eggs make life on a low carb diet easy because they travel well. So, you can carry a hard-boiled egg with you to work for a quick high-fat, low carb afternoon snack. All right, so let's go to walnuts. Now, one of the things I like about walnuts is that they are naturally eaten raw. I recommend all nuts and seeds be eaten raw because when they're roasted, they're typically roasted in an unhealthy vegetable or soybean oil, which wipes out a lot of their benefits. And I'll talk a little bit more about that when I get into the next food. Uh but to stay focused on walnuts about you know 10 12 walnut halves which is about 28 grams of walnuts is all you need in your daily diet to get a tremendous dose of omega-3 fatty acids and a unique blend of antioxidants which are those substances that protect your cells from damage. Um, I will add here that all three of these foods I've talked about so far, avocados, eggs, and walnuts, are also um a great source of B vitamins. And B vitamins help your body convert foods that you eat into energy. So, they help you use calories more efficiently uh, and that sustains your energy throughout the day. Uh, as for how to eat walnuts, well, you can certainly grind them up and put them into recipes, but I find that they make an excellent addition to your salad because the fats in uh walnuts help you absorb the fat soluble vitamins from your salad vegetables. Um, and speaking of healthy fats in u salad, that moves me into my next food, which is full fat salad dressing. And I'm including this here because uh when you go on a diet, it is still one of those taboo items. We think salad and we think low fat dressing. However, putting a full fat dressing on your salad is not something you should be afraid of like walnuts. The fat in dressing helps your body absorb fats soluble vitamins um and also other micronutrients like carotenoids which are the antioxidants that we find in uh bright red uh vegetables and and orange and and uh yellow vegetables. Um so when your salad contains healthy fats, your body is able to pull more of the nutrients out of the salad. And I will tell you that if you have never learned how to make a salad that you truly love to eat, then I encourage you to experiment with adding healthy fats to your salad. Uh if you've been following me for a while, you know the one of my 0123 strategies stands for one large salad each day. Having a salad a day is a great way to jumpstart your healthy weight loss, but if you hate salad, you're not going to go very far. Um, add enough healthy fats to your salad and you will never look back. So, go grab a copy of my free 0123 strategy and then make a salad with avocado, hard-boiled egg, uh, walnuts, and a full fat dressing. Easy, enjoyable, effective, right? Okay. But, uh, there is one caveat I want to mention about full fat dressing, and that is that your dressing should contain a healthy fat. Read the ingredients label and avoid ones with vegetable oil and soybean oil. These are unstable fats and they break down during processing and they become toxic to your body. And uh let me let me save you some time here. It is very hard to find a salad dressing in your grocery store without those oils. I use Primal Kitchen salad dressing. You might be able to find this at your local grocery store. If not, you can find it on my Amazon affiliate shop and I will leave links in the description area below for you. This is made with avocado oil, which is a more stable oil and therefore a healthier fat. All right. And lastly, MCT oil. This oil has come into vogue with the ketogenic diet, which is a very low carb diet. Uh the reason it's linked to a ketogenic diet is because of the unique way it's handled by your body. MCT stands for mediumchain triglyceride. It's a high quality fat that comes mostly from coconut oil. Uh it's a great fuel for for not only your body but also your brain. Uh in your body, MCT oil uh MCTs are easily converted to energy. So when you take them in, they're burned for fuel. Uh and very few of them are stored as fat. So you can obviously see why they're uh oil of interest, right? For those of you that don't know, I have a second YouTube channel that I run with my husband. We recently tested our blood after consuming MCT oil and found that not only does it lower your blood glucose, it also boosts the number of ketones in your blood. So, if you're interested in that video, I will leave links here. Um, MCT oil doesn't have a strong flavor. So, you can add it to salad uh or you can stir it into coffee, which is what we did in in our experiment. So, here you are. Here are five foods that you can easily and deliciously work into your daily diet. - Is Keto TOO Much for You? Do This Instead [Lower-Carb/Better-Carb]: Kind: captions Language: en-US A keto diet is a very low carb diet. The purpose of a keto diet is to encourage your body to produce ketones, which are breakdown products of fat metabolism. Ketones can be measured, and if your body is producing them, you are burning fat. In other words, a keto diet is an effective fat-loss diet. But what if it is too extreme for you? Restricting your carb intake to around 20 or 30 grams per day cuts out a lot of favorite foods and limits fruit and other plant-based foods. So what do you do if keto feels out of reach? You can get fat loss results by simply reducing your carb intake and making better carb choices. This video explains the difference and how you can enhance this less extreme strategy to make fast, healthy progress. [Intro Music] So, low carb vs. keto. What's the difference? Plainly stated, it is simply the number of grams of carbohydrates you consume. While there is no established cut-off point, it is generally accepted that for a person who eats 2,000 calories a day to be considered a low carb dieter, their daily carbohydrate intake needs to be below 125 total grams. To be considered in the keto range, their consumption would drop to less than 50 grams per day. Now, some of you feel that it is splitting hairs to think of a keto diet and a low-carb diet as separate strategies. However, I make that distinction because of the degree of food restrictions from one to the other, which I will go into in a moment. But one thing I have noticed is that we fight a lot about dieting. It seems like picking a diet is like picking a team. So, you are either in camp keto, camp Mediterranean, camp Paleo, or camp vegetarian. And if anyone disagrees with our perspective, we hurl insulting comments at them. Unfortunately, when we focus on the differences, we overlook the similarities. The reality is that all of these diets cut out the same diet destroyer - ultra-processed Because nearly 60% of the calories consumed by Americans are ultra-processed foods, cut them out, and you lose weight and improve your health. So all of these diets are a step in the right direction. Ultra-processed foods include soda, potato chips, sugary cereals, cookies, cakes, candies, chicken nuggets, french fries, ice cream, and many of the toppings and dips that go with them. Not only are these foods the first to go on any healthy diet. Most are also high-carb foods, so cut them out, and you are already headed toward a lower-carb/better-carb diet. Now, when we talk about carbohydrates, the focus is on plant foods for a very good reason. Plants make carbohydrates through photosynthesis. So if it is a plant, it contains carbs. A few animal-based foods, like dairy products, may contain carbohydrates, but for the most part, carb foods are plant foods. This is why a keto diet limits plant food. If you do not want to go to that extreme, then aim to eat a lower-carb/better-carb diet. That is achieved by choosing foods with a favorable fiber-to-carb ratio. I have a blog post that goes into ranking the best to worst carbs, and I will point you to that post if you would like a more in-depth list. But for a quick reference, plant foods with the best fiber-to-carb ratio include non-starchy vegetables, lower-carb fruits, and raw nuts and seeds. So, think about building your diet around a large salad with slices of avocado, berries, nuts, and seeds for one meal and having a cooked non-starchy vegetable, like broccoli, asparagus, or cauliflower, as a side dish alongside a protein entree for another meal. Animal-based foods like meat, fish, and poultry make great entrees because they provide protein and fat without carbs. If you are a breakfast eater, eggs are an additional source of protein and fat. And, what you are doing by building your eating day in this way is continually feeding yourself foods that promote fat loss. To say it more clearly, foods that contain protein, fat, and fiber promote fat loss because they digest slowly and prevent blood glucose and insulin spikes. What that means to you is that your hunger stays under control longer, and the energy from your meal is slowly released, providing a nice, sustained level of energy with less leftover energy to go into fat storage. Then, when you have lower-carb/better-carb food choices under control, you can add weight loss accelerators that may actually allow your body to make ketones. In other words, following a keto diet is not the only way to coax your body into making ketones. Ketones are produced when your body's primary fuel, which is glucose, is running low. A ketogenic diet leads to the production of ketones because there are so few carbs to raise blood glucose. However, any action that sufficiently depletes glucose will result in the production of ketones. These actions include intermittent fasting and exercise. So, by combining your lower carb diet with these actions, you improve fat burning. Some people will find that they must keep their carb intake in the keto range to continue losing weight. But an individual's carb tolerance is not something you can predict, it is something discovered through experience. So if starting a keto diet is intimidating, start with a lower-carb/better-carb diet and go from there. If you would like guidance to get started, download my 0,1,2,3 strategy by clicking on the icon on screen or the link in the description area. The strategy will work with any healthy eating preference because it helps you get sugar and refined foods out of your diet and whole foods in. Thanks for watching, and have a great rest of your day.

- LOW CARB DIETS: 5 benefits of curbing carbs!: Kind: captions Language: en-GB Everyone seems be talking about low carb diets these days! But what exactly is one, and do they work? Today we look at 5 benefits that curbing carbs could bring to you. Let's dive in. Hi, I'm Chris. Welcome to Carbs & Cals, the channel that helps you understand the food you eat so you can manage your diabetes and weight, and lead a healthy lifestyle. If you are new to the channel, hit subscribe and the bell button for all the latest videos. In recent years, low carb diets have received a lot of attention. While there is no agreed definition in healthcare, the media or the general population, consuming 50 to 130g of carbs per day is considered low carb, and less than 50g of carbs per day is very low carb. Many people have found these diets benficial to manage their weight, and some find it easier to manage their diabetes with a lower carb intake. Here are 5 ways you could benefit from following a low carb diet. 1. Going low carb naturally helps you eat less sugar. Some carb foods are processed and high in free sugars and fat. These include biscuits, desserts, sweet pastries, chocolate, sweets and sugary drinks. By curbing your carbs, you may find that you easily avoid these less-beneficial foods. 2. A low carb mindset could lead to a more balanced diet. For example, if half your dinner plate is usually potatoes, rice or pasta, you could reduce these carbs to a quarter of a plate, and add more vegetables. 3. A low carb, high protein intake can help reduce your appetite. This is because protein keeps you feeling fuller for longer than carbs. Like carbs though, some types of protein are more beneficial than others, so aim for good quality protein such as chicken, fish, eggs, beans, nuts and tofu. We'll be making a separate video with 3 nutritious low carb lunches under 15g of carbs. Make sure you subscribe so you don't miss it. 4. Weight loss can be rapid in the early stages of a low carb diet. This is due to the loss of glycogen stored in the body, which is made up of around 25% glucose and 75% water. Therefore, when you embark on a low carb diet, you lose water from the body stores. This initial weight loss can be a great kickstart, but it is important to remember that weight loss tends to slow down after the initial period. And finally, number 5. If you have (or are at risk of developing) diabetes, following a low carb diet can improve blood glucose levels after meals. Some people even find they need less diabetes medication. You can find all the foods from this video, and lots more, in the Carb & Calorie Counter book and app. Links to the full range of bestselling carb counting books are in the description below. So, what's your favourite low carb meal? Share your comments to inspire the Carbs & Cals community. Thanks for watching. We hope you've enjoyed this week's video. If so, give it a thumbs up. New videos released every week, so make sure you subscribe and press the bell. We'll see you next time!

- A Full Day of Keto – Eat This Today! Keto Menu & Recipes: Kind: captions Language: en the keto menu in front of me contains less than 25 total grams of carbohydrates so if you are just getting started with keto you're simply curious about what a keto day of eating looks like or you've hit a weight loss plateau this full day of eating keto has you covered in this video i'll share the easy recipes for a make it yourself breakfast lunch and dinner [Music] as i mentioned the foods in this one day keto menu contain less than 25 total grams of carbohydrates nine of which are fiber now if you are coming from a high carb lifestyle where you were consuming refined carbohydrates like cereal sandwich bread soda pasta and desserts on a regular basis this will be a big change for your body and even if you were eating a healthy high carb diet your daily carb consumption was likely around 50 or more of your daily calories with a keto diet it is a very low carb diet where your carbohydrate intake will be less than 10 percent of your daily calories it will take time for your body to adapt to this way of eating so if you make this switch overnight you may experience keto flu symptoms such as fatigue cravings and irritability to lessen those symptoms you may want to start with a low carb diet before jumping into keto if so you can watch my video that shares a full day of eating low carb okay with that said let's take a look at a quick and satisfying keto breakfast eggs are a favorite food for keto dieters because they provide your body with fat and protein yet contain virtually no carbohydrates eggs can also be enjoyed in a variety of ways you can fry them scramble them or eat them hard-boiled like i am showing in this keto breakfast hard-boiled eggs are good to have on hand if you are following a keto diet because they work as well as a grab-and-go keto snack in the afternoon as they do in the morning and if a plain hard-boiled egg doesn't excite you eating them with a spicy dipping sauce will certainly up the game for instance you can whip up this dipping sauce by mixing together two tablespoons of mayonnaise a teaspoon of dijon mustard 1 8 of a teaspoon of cayenne pepper and a dash of salt and pepper now i will share the ingredients for each of these foods in the video but if you would like to print a copy you can download all of the recipes on my blog post by going to dr beckyfitness.com keto menu and i will put that link in the description area below this video with two hard-boiled eggs this quick keto eggs with spicy dipping sauce recipe contains just over 1 gram of carbohydrates yet it is packed with energy thanks to the 35 grams of fat and more than 12 grams of protein that is a macronutrient ratio that you want as a keto dieter a keto diet is a very low carb high fat moderate protein diet this macronutrient intake prevents the blood sugar and insulin spikes you get when you eat something refined for breakfast like cereal or a muffin when insulin is high it prevents the release of fat from the fat cells so right from the start of your day you have a satisfying meal that keeps your blood sugar and insulin levels low and under control plus if your morning is rushed or you are practicing intermittent fasting in the morning and need a late breakfast that is easy to transport you can make the hard-boiled eggs and dipping sauce the night before and then just grab and go in the morning vegetables can be a part of a keto diet you just have to know which vegetables to choose non-starchy vegetables are lower in carbohydrates than starchy vegetables and i have a video that breaks down which vegetables fit into which category if you want the full breakdown however you can get in the ballpark by thinking of starchy vegetables as those that tend to be heavy and dense like potatoes corn and butternut squash non-starchy vegetables on the other hand are light and airy making them a perfect choice for a salad and this brings us to our lunchtime salad that is included in this keto menu now all varieties of lettuce are low in calories and carbs but they provide you with a lot of hunger satisfying volume for instance this salad has three cups of salad greens that only add 38 calories and four and a half grams of carbs three of which are fiber including a salad in your daily keto diet also supplies you with a perfect vehicle for bringing healthy fats into your daily diet adding fat to your diet helps your body transition away from a primarily carb burning metabolism to a fat burning metabolism so for this salad i added three tablespoons of feta cheese crumbles two tablespoons of walnuts and one tablespoon of raw sunflower seeds and one and a half tablespoons of full fat dressing those salad toppers added more than 30 grams of fat to the day's menu if you are new to keto thinking about getting started or you hit a rut with your keto diet i encourage you to include a daily salad like this one the volume and high fat content will work together to keep hunger away allowing you to go longer between meals and that is something that you want if weight loss is your goal because it allows your blood sugar and insulin levels to drop to a point where fat release from body fat is encouraged any meat or fish will be low in carbohydrates so the great thing about a keto or low carb lifestyle is that you can eat at a restaurant as easily as eating at home for this demonstration i'm using fish specifically cod but i could have just as easily included chicken or steak for this keto meal plan to make this keto cod recipe i preheated the oven to 400 degrees fahrenheit and placed one pound of cod filets in a baking dish with one tablespoon each of avocado oil and lemon juice i then seasoned the fillets with a tablespoon of basil and a dash of salt and pepper and that is really all you need to do with a firm white fish like cod but for this recipe i topped the fillets with slices of tomato and a sprinkle of grated parmesan cheese before placing them in the oven for about 20 minutes and here again you can download all of these recipes by visiting my blog post at drbeckyfitness.com they are free to download and include the metric measurements for those of you outside of the us as well as the calories and macros so you can make this full day of keto eating today if you would like to now one thing about fish is that it is not particularly high in fat each serving of this recipe contains just over 5 grams of fat because my dinner entree is low in fat i am adding fat to it with tartar sauce when you follow a keto diet something you need to look out for is condiments and sauces many of them contain sugar and high fructose corn syrup that can sabotage your weight loss and knock you out of ketosis fortunately you can whip up homemade tartar sauce with just three ingredients that you may already have in your kitchen i made this recipe by stirring together a quarter cup of full fat mayonnaise one tablespoon of dill relish and one teaspoon of lemon juice a serving which is 1 4 of the recipe adds 12 grams of fat to our keto dinner i'm also adding more vegetables and boosting the fat content of this keto meal with bacon roasted brussels sprouts you've heard it said that bacon makes everything better well that is certainly true with brussels sprouts you want to select a good quality bacon that doesn't contain added sugar or similar items like maple syrup because those things will add carbohydrates to your meal to make this recipe i used a pound of brussels sprouts i cut them in half and drizzled a half a tablespoon of avocado oil over them to allow the salt and pepper to stick i then added a quarter pound of chopped bacon to the sprouts and put them in a 450 degree oven for about 30 minutes until they were slightly charred around the edges which is what really makes them delicious like my similar video where i shared a full day of eating a low-carb diet this keto video was made for youtube which is a broad audience the meals in front of me add up to just under 1200 calories which will be low for some but this works well for demonstration purposes because it's easier to add low carb high fat items rather than to subtract them when we break down the macros for this keto day of eating we see that it contains less than 25 total grams of carbohydrates making up about eight percent of the daily calories with just over 20 percent coming from protein and more than 70 percent of the day's calories coming from fat many of you will find this macronutrient breakdown allows you to lose weight and get into ketosis which is that state in which your body releases body fat to produce ketones however just eating keto for one day will not give you those results when you switch from a high carb diet to a keto diet you force your body to run on fatty acids and ketones rather than this transition is great but it takes time the world we live in is filled with carbohydrates so without a plan it can be hard to avoid temptations and stick with a keto diet long enough to gain the benefits if you would like to have a plan to follow you can check out my 21 day keto challenge during the challenge you'll eat a keto diet with daily menus containing no more than 25 total carbs you also get daily busy day plans and additional grab and go emergency suggestions so you can stay on track even when life gets too busy to cook and i will provide links to my 21 day keto challenge here in the video and below in the description area please subscribe to my channel and hit the bell icon to get a notification when my next video comes out thanks so much for watching and enjoy the rest of your day

- Anti Inflammatory Diet - Sample Menu & Recipes [Low Carb and Keto]: Kind: captions Language: en-US Inflammation is part of your body's natural immune response. However, there is a difference between the short-term inflammatory response that happens when your body is healing a cut or infection and long-term chronic inflammation resulting from a poor diet. Chronic inflammation is the root cause of many diseases and starts to show itself in a variety of ways. If you are bothered by aches, pains, fatigue, depression, anxiety, and unexplained weight gain, an anti-inflammatory diet is worth your while. This video shows you which foods to eat and how to put them together for a full day of eating. [Intro Music] If we peel away all of the layers to get to the root cause of diet-related inflammation, we see that much of the problem lies with processed, packaged, or fast foods and sweetened drinks, like soda. These items are easy and convenient, but you cannot outrun the health consequences of a diet focused on them. No one's diet is perfect, but by switching your food choices to those that limit inflammation, you give your body what it needs, and in return, you get the health and weight control you want. Foods that contain antioxidants, vitamins and minerals, unrefined carbohydrates, monounsaturated fats, and omega-3 fatty acids are at the top of the list when it comes to anti-inflammatory foods. These foods include things like Vegetables, Berries, Avocados, Raw Nuts & Seeds, Extra Virgin Oils, Wild-caught Fish, Spices, Tea, and Dark Chocolate. From this list, we can create a sample menu for a full day of eating. In the morning, you can break your overnight fast with a Pepper and Mushroom Omelet A Salad topped with Salmon, Walnuts, and Vegetables makes a good anti-inflammatory lunch. If a snack is desired, a Blueberry & Avocado Smoothie provides a rich dose of antioxidants. You can round out your day with a dinner of Baked Chicken with Homemade Mexican Cauliflower Rice. And, a couple of squares of Dark Chocolate and a cup of Green Tea allow you to enjoy dessert without promoting inflammation. Let's go over how to make this menu doable and how you are benefitting from each of these You can start your day with a two-egg omelet filled with a quarter cup each of chopped green pepper and mushrooms; cooked in a tablespoon of avocado oil. Peppers and mushrooms contain antioxidants. Antioxidants are compounds that protect your cells from being damaged by unstable molecules called free radicals. Cooking your omelet in a heat-resistant oil, like avocado oil, adds to the anti-inflammatory effect that you are after. When cooking, you want to avoid unstable, polyunsaturated oils, like vegetable oil or soybean oil, because they degrade when exposed to heat. Choosing extra virgin oil is also a step in the right direction. Extra virgin means that the oil is from the first extraction, which contains more antioxidants and helpful compounds than regular oil. Eggs, by themselves, are not classified as anti-inflammatory foods but can be included in the day's menu for most people. If eggs are not something you tolerate, you can swap the omelet for the smoothie or skip breakfast to practice intermittent fasting. I will discuss the smoothie after we look at lunch. Also, I put all of these recipes together for you in a blog post. So, if you want to create this menu in your home, you can download the recipes at DrBeckyFitness.com/anti-inflammatory-menu For lunch, you can enjoy a large salad topped with five ounces of wild-caught salmon, a couple of tablespoons of walnuts, tomatoes, and assorted non-starchy vegetables. For salad dressing, a simple olive oil and vinegar dressing can be mixed up at home. The typical ratio for oil to vinegar is 3 to 1. For instance, you can mix three teaspoons of oil with one teaspoon of vinegar for a quick salad dressing. For a bit of flavor, I like to stir in a small amount of Dijon mustard. Salmon is a fatty fish that provides a good source of inflammation preventing omega-3 fatty acids. If you are not much for cooking or looking for a quick lunch, you can use packaged wild-caught salmon. Walnuts not only enhance the flavor and crunch of the salad, but they also provide healthy fats that help your body absorb the fat-soluble vitamins in the vegetables. Tomatoes are high in lycopene, which is an antioxidant that gets a lot of praise for its anti-inflammatory properties. For vegetables, you can choose any non-starchy vegetables that you enjoy. Chopped broccoli and dark leafy greens are good choices. Broccoli is a type of cruciferous vegetable. Vegetables from this family have been shown to play a role in cancer prevention. Dark leafy greens, such as kale or spinach, are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals but low in calories. So, you can fill a large salad bowl with 2 to 4 cups of leafy greens to get a lot of satisfying volume that will take a long time to digest, keeping hunger away. If hunger returns, you can fuel your body with an anti-inflammatory smoothie. A smoothie is a fun food that you can pack with beneficial ingredients. For instance, you can whip up an anti-inflammatory smoothie by placing half of an avocado and a half cup of frozen blueberries into a blender along with a cup of almond milk, a handful of spinach, and a tablespoon of almond butter. Almond milk is a non-dairy milk. Dairy is not an issue for everyone, but some people do not tolerate it, so we are omitting dairy from this anti-inflammatory sample menu. Almond butter can be included, but avoid almond butter with added sugar or soybean oil. The two anti-inflammatory stars of this smoothie are avocado and berries. Avocados are a great source of healthy fats and minerals that your body needs, like potassium and magnesium. Berries are a rich source of inflammation-fighting antioxidants. They are also lower in carbohydrates than many fruits, making berries a good way to add a touch of sweetness to a low carb diet. Our sample menu dinner included 6 ounces of chicken with a one cup serving of seasoned cauliflower rice. Like eggs, chicken is not classified as an anti-inflammatory food but can work for your full day of eating. When choosing an entr e for an anti-inflammatory diet, higher quality meats are the best choice. If it is feasible for you, look for grass-fed meats with no hormones or antibiotics and avoid convenience meats, like lunch meats, hot dogs, and similar processed items. Dinner does not need to be complicated. Chicken can be seasoned with salt and pepper and baked in the oven. If you do not have time to cook, you can pick up a pre-cooked rotisserie chicken at your local grocery store. For a side dish, you can prepare Mexican cauliflower rice on the stove. Cauliflower is a cruciferous vegetable, like broccoli that we discussed earlier. For convenience, you can pick up a one-pound bag of frozen cauliflower rice at your local grocery store and cook it with a couple of tablespoons of avocado oil to get it tender. Cauliflower rice can be spiced up in many ways. For this side dish, I used a homemade taco seasoning mix with added turmeric. Most spices contain compounds that benefit your health. Turmeric gets extra attention because it is a source of an inflammation-fighting antioxidant called curcumin. You can download this Mexican cauliflower rice recipe and all of the recipes I have discussed for free on my website DrBeckyFitness.com/anti-inflammatory-menu. A couple of squares of Dark Chocolate and a cup of tea can be enjoyed to wind down after dinner. Dark chocolate contains antioxidants. However, the chocolate must contain at least 70% cacao and limit added sugar to reap the health benefits. If your dark chocolate bar has sugar listed as one of the top three ingredients, it does not give you the inflammatory protection you are after. Tea is a non-caloric drink that gets a lot of praise as a healthy beverage. Green tea is the most extensively studied tea for its beneficial effects on inflammatory diseases. Its praise is partly due to the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of compounds in green tea, like EGCG. If you watch my channel, you have heard me refer to tea as a stopper. A stopper is a food, drink, or activity that allows you to separate from the act of eating. Hot tea takes a long time to sip, allowing your body time to tell your brain that you are full. Therefore, having a cup of tea after dinner is not only a way to control inflammation but also a way to prevent overeating. This full day of eating provides just over 1,500 calories and only 56 grams of carbohydrates, which works perfectly for low carb dieters. It is a sample menu, to show you how to combine anti-inflammatory foods. The calories can be adapted to suit your needs by changing the portion sizes. All of the recipes are available on my blog. You can visit DrBeckyFitness.com/anti-inflammatory-menu. I hope this helps you jumpstart healthy eating. For more videos like this, be sure to subscribe to my channel before you go. Have a great day.

- Zero Carb Food List that Keeps Keto and Ketosis Simple: Kind: captions Language: en The one golden rule in a keto diet is to keep carbs low. This doesn't just mean cutting out bread, pasta and grains, it means truly giving your eating habits a hard reset. Perhaps one of the most daunting elements of the keto diet for people can be the idea that they have to count every single carb. We do encourage you to track your macros and be mindful of your carb intake. But there is one simple strategy that helps to clear up any confusion around how much of what foods you should and shouldn't be eating: choose mostly keto-friendly foods that have little to no net carbs. There are so many low - and no-carb options, you have hundreds of delicious options to choose from! To help simplify things and make your keto journey as easy as possible, we've put together the ultimate list of zero-carb foods for you to enjoy. Before we begin, it's important to note that nearly every kind of food has some sort of trace carbohydrate content. When we say "zero carbs," we are talking about foods and beverages that contain less than one gram of net carbs per serving, and foods that aren't usually consumed in large enough quantities to negatively affect your daily carb consumption. Now, let's take a look at zero-carb foods, starting with meats: Beef, fowl and pork are familiar meats that provide you with protein. Less common types of meats, like veal, lamb, game meats, including venison and elk, and exotic meats, can help add variety and interest to your meals. Organ meats can be a good choice, too, but just be aware that liver contains glycogen, a source of net carbs. When possible, seek out grass-fed and pasture-raised meats. While it's typically best to avoid processed meats, cured and dried meats, like bacon, salami, hot dogs, deli meats, jerky and canned meats are fan favorites on the keto diet that can used on occasion. However, you must read the ingredients list and put back anything containing dextrose, starch, sugar, high fructose corn syrup, or maltodextrin. Always try to buy all-natural processed meats that contain carb-free ingredients. Fresh and tinned seafood, many of which contain beneficial omega-3 fatty acid, are another great source of protein. White flaky fish like cod, flounder, sole and haddock, are tasty, as are fattier, more flavorful fish, like salmon, tuna, halibut, swordfish, sardines, Spanish mackerel, trout and others. We've found that the healthiest option is to buy wild caught salmon and sardines, canned or fresh without any other ingredients. While we recommend against frequent and habitual snacking, we all need snacks from time to time. Even though so many on-the-go bites you once relied on are now off the table, there are so many keto snacks to take their place! Of the bunch, those with the lowest carbs include: pepperoni slices, keto pork rinds, bone broth, smoked fish, canned fish, canned meat, preserved meat products, like jerky, biltong, pemmican and meat sticks, and seaweed. For a more comprehensive look at good keto snacking habits, check out the Ruled.me article titled, "50+ Healthy Keto Snacks to Help with Ketosis." Healthy fats are the cornerstone of a balanced keto diet. Zero carb fats, like extra-virgin olive oil, grass-fed butter and ghee, coconut oil, MCT oil, avocado oil, and animal fats (like lard) are ingredients you should be reaching for at almost every meal. Vegetable, seed, and nut-based oils are unstable when exposed to high heat and can release toxins. We suggest avoiding these. Your spice cabinet is likely packed with flavor enhancers that are virtually carb-free! Avoid any spice blends with added sugars, of course, and instead, try salt and black pepper, dried oregano, rosemary, thyme, basil, chives and dill. Chili powder, curry powder and cinnamon may contain higher traces of carbs and should be used a bit more sparingly. Some condiments, like sugar-free hot sauces and mustard can also add tons of flavor with hardly any carbs at all. While all sugars are cut out from a keto diet, there are plenty of ways to add a hint of sweetness to the dishes we prepare. Zero-carb sweeteners include liquid stevia, monk fruit extract or blends, erythritol and liquid sucralose. Just be sure to choose products, either liquid or solid, that contain no other additives like maltodextrin. For a more comprehensive list of sweeteners, watch our video on keto-friendly sweeteners. Zero-carb drinks are available almost anywhere you go! There is water and sparkling water, of course, as well as black coffee and unsweetened tea. Sugar-free and carb-free energy drinks, diet drink mixes or drink enhancers, and diet soda and other diet drinks, without any added sugar, also count. Keep in mind that for some people, drinking sweeter drinks can trigger sugar cravings in the long-term. When it comes to alcohol, those following the keto diet need to proceed carefully. Some alcoholic drinks have virtually no net carbs, but alcohol will slow fat burning while your body processes it. We suggest limiting your alcohol intake to one drink per day and choosing spirits like gin, unflavored rum, unflavored vodka, whiskey, tequila, scotch, bourbon, brandy or cognac. Always be careful about added carbs in the alcohol you are drinking. For more on keto drinks, see the Ruled.me article, "Complete Guide to Keto Drinks and Beverages." We hope that our guide to zero-carb foods helps to clarify some of the best things you should be reaching for as you continue on your keto journey. For more information and resources on what you should and shouldn't eat on a keto diet, visit Ruled.me and check out the guide titled, "Ketogenic Diet Food List: Everything You Need to Know" alongside our other videos. Thanks for so much for watching!

One of my favorite highmaintenance habits to stay lowmaintenance is laser hair removal at home. And I've been using the Ulike Air10 for my at home treatments. This uses IPL or intense pulseed light, which is a clinically approved hair removal method that targets the melanin inside hair follicles. It uses a powerful and safe spectrum of light to reduce visible hair without damaging the skin. Because this works by targeting melanin, its effectiveness is based on the ratio between melanin in the skin and in the hair. I absolutely love IPL compared to other hair removal methods because it is so quick, so painless, and the results are super long-lasting. I used to only shave, but I've cut myself so many times with a razor and then I'll get those stubborn ingrown hairs after. And because I have pretty thick hair, I will literally start to see the hair grow back the same day. It's like, damn, can I just get 24 hours with no hair? Like, is that so much to ask for? I've done waxing, too, but it can be pretty painful, especially if you have a lower pain tolerance. I've also done traditional laser hair removal, but it's so expensive and you have to physically go to a clinic multiple times to see results. Like, I can barely feel it. and it is super quick and easy to use. Of course, with any beauty device, consistency is key. Similar to laser, IPL works gradually. It does not matter if you have three products in your routine or a hundred. If you're not using the right ones or applying them consistently, you're not going to see the results you want. It took a lot of trial and error to figure out what works for my skin. But once I did, I stopped trying all the new stuff and I just stuck with my current lineup. High maintenance tip number five is scalp massages. Listen, scalp care in general is severely underrated. Everyone only cares about their hair, but your hair originates from your scalp. So, we should really be taking care of this first. If you want to have healthier, thicker, shinier hair, you need to massage your scalp. Massaging your head increases blood flow, which nourishes the follicle and makes your hair grow. It also just relieves tension and headaches, which I experience very frequently. You can just use your hands to massage, but I like using this silicone scalp massager in the shower. I will shampoo my hair first and then use this all over and really work the product in. And I noticed that my hair just stays cleaner for longer when I use this. Oh, it just feels so good. I really needed this. Let me tell you, a healthy scalp is truly the secret to loweffort good hair days. Speaking of hair, I am also a big advocate for blow drying it after you wash it. I consider this high maintenance because I feel like most people just let their hair air dry, and I used to do that, too. But air drying leaves your hair wet for long periods of time, making it more prone to damage, frizz, and dryness. Now, before you say that using heat on your hair is bad, not always. Heat damage depends on how much heat you're applying to your hair. So, yeah, if your curling iron is 500°, of course, it's going to damage your hair. But blow drying from a distance will actually make your hair shinier, smoother, and last longer between washes. When I used to air dry my hair, I noticed that it would get greasier a lot faster. But now when I blow dry, I can actually go 2 to 3 days without having to rewash or restyle it. I also have a very frizzy hair type. Like it's straight, but it's puffy at the same time. I don't know how to explain it. So to me, blow drying has helped just maintain the frizz. And once my hair is tame, my life just feels so much easier and I can focus on other things. Number seven is teeth whitening. As some of you know by now, I take my oral hygiene very seriously because even if you brush your teeth, floss, all that good stuff. If you got butter, yellow teeth, people are just going to assume your breath stink. Other things I do to keep my teeth white is I just brush them properly with an electric toothbrush. And I stay away from coffee, wine, or smoking because those can all stain your teeth. Okay, this next beauty habit is very, very niche, but I promise it will change your life. Number eight is wearing mouth tape to sleep. This is both a beauty tip and a sleep tip. If you're like me and you normally sleep with your mouth wide open, this is what I learned about that. Sleeping with your mouth open can cause mouth dryness, bacterial growth, bad breath, and gum disease. On top of that, if your mouth is a gape for 8 hours every day, this will affect how your facial bones develop, and you might give yourself a receding chin. So, mouth taping at night forces you to breathe through your nose, and this helps with sleep quality, reduces morning puffiness, improves facial posture, and even makes your skin look better long term. I know this is a very weird highmaintenance habit, but I've been doing it for a couple months now, and I do notice that my face looks a lot more snatched. Plus, I've also stopped drooling, so thank God. Number nine is red light therapy. This habit definitely feels the most extra and bougie. like you will look like an alien. But red light therapy has so many positive benefits for the skin. It is talked about in every single wellness podcast, magazine, blog, and it's scientifically backed. So again, this is not a woo concept. How it works is that red light carries specific wavelengths of red and infrared light to the skin. And these wavelengths penetrate the skin and interact with cell mitochondria, which then trigger a bunch of biological responses. Some of these responses include stimulating collagen, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing. And when you use a serum with red light therapy, all those antioxidants and vitamins are able to penetrate deeper into the skin and have a stronger effect. There are so many LED masks on the market now, but not all of them are high quality or effective. So, if you're going to do it, you want to get one that has the right wavelengths. For red light, it's usually like 600 to 650 nanome. And you have to make sure that it has enough LED bulbs in the mask itself. Basically, the more LEDs, the more effective light coverage you'll have. My favorite masks are the current body one and then the Shark Cryoglow mask. I'll also link these below if you're interested in checking them out, but I'm not being sponsored by them. I just love to share my recommendations with you. Number 10 is a minimal daily makeup routine. I consider makeup high maintenance because doing a full beat every day is kind of crazy to me. But just a minimal everyday makeup routine that you can do in 5 minutes will seriously elevate your look and make you feel more put together. But the secret to getting that true clean girl look and not clogging your pores is in the products you use. Obviously, you don't need to use a full coverage foundation every day. Just a tinted moisturizer, a little bit of concealer, brow gel, and lip balm is enough. Trust me, this will make it look like you spent so much more time on your face than you actually did. And honestly, just taking a little bit of time for yourself can totally transform how you feel. Like to be real, I do feel more confident and put together when I have a little bit of makeup on. I just feel more prepared and ready for my day. So find the right products for you and curate your own 5-minute routine. But if you don't like wearing makeup or you don't want to wear makeup everyday, then don't do it. Obviously, you know what's best for you. The secret to looking lowmaintenance is not about doing the most every day, but it's actually putting in a little bit of effort ahead of time so that your everyday routine can feel effortless. **If you're doing full body 3x per week or an upper/lower split where you do each one twice per week with four days total, that is more than enough. High intensity with only 1-2 reps shy of failure and moderate volume.

2) If you're training legs 2-3x per week as part of a full body or an upper/lower with compound lifts, the sled, etc you're getting plenty of cardio as is. 

**Daily brisk walking is a great counterpart to lifting over formal zone 2 cardio and sprints. It won't impede lifting, speeds up recovery, and can be done every day year round. Stacking 10-15K steps over  each day will yield serious calorie burn long-term.

- Night Time Routine 🌃 | A Cozy 5PM to 10PM Vlog: Unpacking Groceries, Dinner Prep, & Waffles: Kind: captions Language: en Good evening! I bought quite a bit of groceries today It's been raining a lot these days and the humidity is very high Because of this, washing my hands after returning home feels extra good The presence of daffodils at the grocery store is a clear indication that Spring is just around the corner Flowers are one of those small things that can make me happy for days Poppies are my favorite flowers ~ they bloom like living masterpieces, each one a true work of art I like to quickly "prepare" my groceries once I get home Cutting the butter into small pieces will make it so much more convenient to use when you need it However, I don't use separators between pieces (I am a "little" frugal in that regard haha (⸝⸝ᵕᴗᵕ⸝⸝)) As long as you leave a bit of space between the pieces, they won't stick together even if they fall For vegetables, I like to clean them and then let them dry before storing them in the fridge Dirt trapped in the vegetables can carry bacteria which may lead to contamination Berries are Koko's favourite fruit, so I usually buy a lot and wash them all at once I give them a quick rinse and then soak them in baking soda water for 15 minutes Baking soda helps to remove dirt, pesticides, and other residues effectively You can leave the berries to air dry, but it usually takes really long, so I like to use a salad spinner I promise it's not just because the salad spinner would otherwise sit unused... 𓏗-𓏗 For proteins, I like to remove them from their packaging as soon as I get home It's a habit my mother-in-law taught me long ago, and it really stuck with me She mentioned that this helps reduce the chances of plastic leaching into the food, and I've followed her advice ever since Every Friday afternoon, 🐢 takes 🐨 out to play, so I have some time for myself before dinner I've been loving rooibos and rose tea lately I think they complement each other perfectly ~ The aroma is very delicate, sweet, and soothing ~ Pouring it into the biggest mug I have 😅 The flavour is so rich and mellow at the same time, and so comforting ~ I brewed extra tea to enjoy later We've been eating out a lot lately with relatives visiting, so I've really been craving 'home food' I reckon 'home food' is different for everyone, but for me, it’s either pasta or rice with stir-fried dishes I guess it’s a blend of my Chinese heritage and being born and raised in Italy What's your version of 'home food'? Tonight, I'm going to be making Chinese style food ~ Boiling the pork with a bit of ginger for 5 minutes helps to remove any unpleasant odors For the sauce, I'm using 1/2 tbsp tamari, 1 tbsp oyster sauce, and 1/2 tbsp sugar Fry the ginger in a bit of oil until fragrant Then add in your protein of choice and fry until golden brown Finally, add the vegetables and sauce Many combinations of proteins and vegetables can work with this recipe (Can you tell Koko came home? 😆) The second dish (Koko's favourite dish) is a child's favourite I have so many memories of eating steamed eggs mixed with rice when I was a kid Typically, you'd sift the eggs like I'm doing here to achieve the smoothest texture However, since we're at home (and eggs are expensive), I just use them as they are I like to steam the mixture for 5 minutes before adding my toppings and steaming for 6 more minutes (I got distracted and steamed them for 8 minutes before adding the toppings, but it was fine) Lastly, I'll boil some romaine lettuce In reality, I've been multitasking all the dishes, but I think it's easier to watch this way? 😆 The romaine lettuce is ready to be served after about 30 seconds I'm going to make a quick sauce with a bit of garlic, soy sauce, and sugar Serving dinner with some mixed purple rice ~ 🐢&🐨:"Thanks Mamma" 🐨:"Steamed egg, steamed egg 🎶" It's so delicious ~ Rice killers for sure After dinner, it's time to get Koko ready for bed Lately, we've been showering together for efficiency It also makes story time cosier and more enjoyable for the both of us since we can cuddle together ~ 🐻:"Why is he happy?" 🐨:"Because he ate so much food!" 😆 🐨:"He ate lots of rice! and steamed eggs!" We used to read the same books to her when she was a baby, but in a very different manner These days, we talk about why the characters may be feeling a certain way 🐨:"We can help her put on a bandaid and she'll feel happy!" After reading some stories, we usually cuddle for a few more minutes before she sleeps in her own bed After hearing much about the benefits of red light, I recently bought a red light mask 👍 I'm usually very good at taking it slow, but there are 2 times where I can't! When brushing my teeth and doing a mask 😆 I feel like I have to keep myself busy I purchased a brush cleaner from Amazon and I thought I'd use this time to clean my brushes When did you last wash your brushes? I don't remember the last time I washed mine.. I added a bit (too much) of makeup brush cleaning solution The water is turning murky just from dipping the brush 🙂 This is actually incredibly fun and, most importantly, very fast! There's still some foam residue, so I rinse it off and then spin it dry It's actually very dry and fluffy after spinning ~ And it also comes with a spot to air dry and different attachments I'm very happy with this purchase ! (And so is my skin! Haha ) I'm going to prepare yeast waffles for tomorrow's breakfast Combine 190g flour, 4g yeast, 80g milk, 1 egg, 35g of sugar, and a bit of vanilla extract Once the dough is formed, add in 30g of butter and combine Cover and store in the fridge overnight From 9 to 10 it's usually time for 🐢and I to catch up on our day It's basically the only hour we have for the two of us haha We enjoy some tea and toast to the fact that we did a great job today once again! 😆 I divided the dough into 6 parts to make 6 waffles Yeast waffles are so cute and fluffy ✨ I'm going to top the waffles with some greek yoghurt If you add some matcha powder into the yoghurt, you'll get a delicious matcha sauce ! Time to make it pretty ~ The weather calls for an iced latte ☺️ It feels like I'm at a cafe ☕️ Waffles go very well with coffee I hope you enjoyed watching this video as much as I enjoyed making it
